memberdef.name: OptionsTable

briefdescription.para: This function sets the options for the SPI device driver.

detaileddescription.para: The options control how the device behaves relative to the SPI bus. The device must be idle rather than busy transferring data before setting these device options.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpiPs instance.

contains the specified options to be set. This is a bit mask where a 1 means to turn the option on, and a 0 means to turn the option off. One or more bit values may be contained in the mask. See the bit definitions named XSPIPS_*_OPTIONS in the file xspips.h.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if options are successfully set.
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the device is currently transferring data. The transfer must complete or be aborted before setting options.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the options for the SPI device.

detaileddescription.para: The options control how the device behaves relative to the SPI bus.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XSpiPs instance.
Options contains the specified options currently set. This is a bit value where a 1 means the option is on, and a 0 means the option is off. See the bit definitions named XSPIPS_*_OPTIONS in file xspips.h.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the clock prescaler for an SPI device.

detaileddescription.para: The device must be idle rather than busy transferring data before setting these device options.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpiPs instance.
is the value that determine how much the clock should be divided by. Use the XSPIPS_CLK_PRESCALE_* constants defined in xspips.h for this setting.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if options are successfully set.
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the device is currently transferring data. The transfer must complete or be aborted before setting options.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the clock prescaler of an SPI device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpiPs instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (const XSpiPs *InstancePtr, u8 DelayNss, u8 DelayBtwn, u8 DelayAfter, u8 DelayInit)
memberdef.name: XSpiPs_SetDelays

briefdescription.para: This function sets the delay register for the SPI device driver.

detaileddescription.para: The delay register controls the Delay Between Transfers, Delay After Transfers, and the Delay Initially. The default value is 0x0. The range of each delay value is 0-255.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpiPs instance.
is the delay for which the chip select outputs will be de-asserted between words when CPHA=0.
is the delay between one Slave Select being de-activated and the activation of another slave. The delay is the number of master clock periods given by DelayBtwn + 2.

define the delay between the last bit of the current byte transfer and the first bit of the next byte transfer. The delay in number of master clock periods is given as: CPHA=0:DelayInit+DelayAfter+3 CPHA=1:DelayAfter+1
is the delay between asserting the slave select signal and the first bit transfer. The delay int number of master clock periods is DelayInit+1.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if delays are successfully set.
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the device is currently transferring data. The transfer must complete or be aborted before setting options.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the delay settings for an SPI device.

detaileddescription.para: The delay register controls the Delay Between Transfers, Delay After Transfers, and the Delay Initially. The default value is 0x0.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpiPs instance.
is a pointer to the delay for which the chip select outputs will be de-asserted between words when CPHA=0.
is a pointer to the Delay Between transfers value. This is a return parameter.
is a pointer to the Delay After transfer value. This is a return parameter.
is a pointer to the Delay Initially value. This is a return parameter.
